request.question:
Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.
In plain English: can a drug already used in transplant patients slow or reverse cellular aging in a dish?

hypothesis.threshold: at least 40% lower than vehicle controls
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific; hypothesis is clear and detailed.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: rapamycin IMR-90 fibroblasts p21 senescence mTORC1
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.
In plain English: can a drug already used in transplant patients slow or reverse cellular aging in a dish?

references.title: mTOR regulates MAPKAPK2 translation to control the senescence-associated secretory phenotype
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses mTOR's role in translating signals that affect senescence, potentially overlapping with the effects of rapamycin as an mTOR inhibitor.

references.title: Regulation of ornithine decarboxylase and other cell cycle-dependent genes during senescence of IMR-90 human diploid fibroblasts.
references.relevance_note: This study specifically evaluates cellular aging in IMR-90 fibroblasts, which aligns closely with the biological system of interest.

references.title: Decrease of p400 ATPase complex and loss of H2A.Z within the p21 promoter occur in senescent IMR-90 human fibroblasts.
references.relevance_note: This paper investigates changes that occur in the p21 promoter in senescent IMR-90 fibroblasts, which may relate to the outcomes of interest in the proposed study.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of rapamycin is related to the broader category of mTOR inhibitors, which may relate to cellular senescence in IMR-90 fibroblasts as indicated by the work on MAPKAPK2 and the senescence-associated secretory phenotype (abstract of paper [1]). IMR-90 fibroblasts are the same biological system, and while there is no direct mention of rapamycin's effect on p21-positive senescent cells, the context of cellular aging is consistent with the aims of the proposed study. Papers [2] and [4] provide additional insights into the senescence of IMR-90 fibroblasts, indicating relevant research exists on senescence mechanisms in this cell line.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.description: Standard translational risk remains even with literature support. Run a pilot before moving to full execution.
risks.mitigation: Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: Determine the effect of 100 nM rapamycin on the number of p21-positive senescent IMR-90 fibroblasts compared to vehicle controls.
plan.experimental_design: IMR-90 fibroblasts will be treated with either 100 nM rapamycin or a vehicle control for 72 hours. Following treatment, cells will be fixed and stained for p21 expression using immunofluorescence, and the proportion of p21-positive cells will be quantified using fluorescence microscopy.
controls: 100 nM rapamycin treatment, expected to decrease p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10 mL of 100 nM rapamycin solution by diluting 10 µL of a 10 mM rapamycin stock in 10 mL of complete DMEM culture medium (Gibco, 11965-092).
Expected outcome: Complete preparation of the rapamycin treatment solution.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Cell Culture
Seed 1 × 10^5 IMR-90 fibroblasts in a 6-well plate containing 2 mL of complete DMEM per well.
Expected outcome: Cells attached and actively proliferating after 4-6 h.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Treatment
Treat half of the wells with 2 mL of 100 nM rapamycin solution and the other half with 2 mL of vehicle control.
Expected outcome: Cells exposed to either rapamycin or vehicle control for 72 h.

DAY 1 | 72 h | Incubation
Incubate the cells at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 72 h.
Expected outcome: Cells remain healthy and viable throughout the treatment period.

DAY 4 | 30 min | Fixation
Fix cells by adding 1 mL of 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS to each well and incubating at room temperature for 30 min.
Expected outcome: Cells are fixed and ready for staining.

DAY 4 | 20 min | Immunofluorescence Staining
Remove fixation solution and wash cells with 1 mL of PBS three times for 5 min each.
Expected outcome: Cells prepared for immunostaining.

DAY 4 | 16 h | Immunofluorescence Staining
Incubate cells with 200 µL of primary anti-p21 antibody (1:100 dilution in PBS; Cell Signaling, 2947) at 4°C overnight.
Expected outcome: Specific binding of anti-p21 antibody to target cells.

DAY 5 | 1 h | Immunofluorescence Staining
Wash cells with PBS three times for 5 min each, then incubate with 200 µL of secondary antibody (1:200 dilution; Alexa Fluor, A-11034) for 1 h at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Fluorescent signal from secondary antibody bound to p21.

DAY 5 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze the stained cells using a fluorescence microscope (Leica DM6000) and quantify the percentage of p21-positive cells using ImageJ software.
Expected outcome: Quantify the percentage of p21-positive cells with a minimum of 200 cells counted per treatment condition.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Rapamycin effectively inhibits mTORC1 in IMR-90 fibroblasts at the specified concentration.
p21 expression can be accurately quantified using immunofluorescence staining methods.
Cell viability is maintained despite treatment with rapamycin.
decision_criteria: Proceed if the percentage of p21-positive cells in the rapamycin group is at least 40% lower than in the vehicle control group.
Assure that p21 staining shows clear and distinct fluorescent signals.
Confirm that cell viability remains above 90% post-treatment.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot number of rapamycin for all treatments to ensure consistency.
Maintain consistent passage number of IMR-90 cells across all experiments.
Calibrate the fluorescence microscope before the analysis.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 40% lower than vehicle controls improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine the effect of 100 nM rapamycin on the number of p21-positive senescent IMR-90 fibroblasts compared to vehicle controls.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
